channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House GOP leaders prep vote on budget despite rift within the party
item.description: Republicans in Congress are closer to passing key elements of President Trump's legislative agenda — like extending tax cuts that expire at the end of the year — but only if the House and Senate can get on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Immigration judge to rule Friday on possible release of detained Columbia activist Mahmoud Khalil
item.description: An immigration judge in Louisiana says she will decide later this week whether the government can continue to detain Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia University activist facing deportation for his role in pro-Palestinian campus protests.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline And Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Internal Revenue Service agrees to send immigrant tax data to ICE for enforcement
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Internal Revenue Service has agreed to share immigrants' tax data with Immigration and Customs Enforcement for the purpose of identifying and deporting people illegally in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee nomination advances in Senate, one step closer to becoming ambassador to Israel
item.description: The Senate moved closer to confirming Mike Huckabee as ambassador to Israel amid backlash over his past remarks on Palestinians and Trump’s vision for the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lombardo's crime bill focuses on harsher penalties in Nevada, penalizing repeat offenders
item.description: Republican Nevada Gov. Joe Lombardo is proposing harsher penalties for repeat offenders, DUI drivers and other criminals as part of his sweeping crime bill introduced Monday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump crypto chief says we are in the 'golden age' for digital assets, 'clearing the deck' of Biden barriers
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: President Trump wants to make the United States the “crypto capital of the world," and a top White House cryptocurrency policy official told Fox News Digital the administration is well on its way to ushering in “the golden age for digital assets.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Dem accuses Trump admin of 'fire sale approach' as GOP aims to lower federal gov's real estate portfolio
item.description: Democrats accused the Trump administration of "looting" the federal government during a Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) subcommittee hearing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Legal status revoked for 985,000 migrants who entered US under Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is terminating parole protections for people who entered the country through the CBP One app, revoking legal status for nearly a million migrants who came to the U.S. during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court lets Trump move forward with firing thousands of federal workers
item.description: The Supreme Court's stay, which allows the administration to execute the firings for now while it litigates in federal court, does not mean the terminations were lawful.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg and Christina Gatti, MPR News

item.title: House Republicans worry even Trump can't save them as tax cuts, budget bill hang in balance
item.description: House Republicans are bracing for a rebellion over legislation to move Trump's agenda through Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court blocks order requiring Trump administration to reinstate thousands of federal workers
item.description: The Supreme Court has blocked an order for the Trump administration to return to work thousands of federal employees who were let go in mass firings aimed at dramatically downsizing the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: SCOOP: Treasury Secretary Bessent to huddle with key Republican caucus as Trump enacts tariff plan
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will be on Capitol Hill on Wednesday speaking with House Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Park Service restores original Harriet Tubman, Underground Railroad webpage
item.description: The National Park Service has reversed edits and restored content to its webpage about Harriet Tubman and the Underground Railroad in the wake of news reports and public backlash over the changes.
item.dc:creator: Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: The Supreme Court has allowed the Trump administration to use a 1798 wartime law, the Alien Enemies Act, to deport Venezuelan migrants it accuses of being gang members, ending the temporary halt on deportations ordered by a federal district judge.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Navy chief 'regrets' costly missile interceptors against Houthis, pushes for cheaper Red Sea defense
item.description: Adm. James Kilby says expensive Red Sea missile defenses are not sustainable and calls on the military-industrial base to speed up cheaper munitions production.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans rattled by tariff troubles begin to push back on Trump
item.description: Cracks are growing between congressional Republicans and President Trump over his tariff policy, with some lawmakers looking to strip him of his unilateral tariff authority and others seeking briefings from the White House on the strategy amid growing economic tumult.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Spanberger raises $6.7 million in Virginia governor race
item.description: Former Rep. Abigail Spanberger’s (D-Va.) gubernatorial campaign said Monday it raised $6.7 million in the first quarter of the year. The haul included more than 50,000 donations of $100 or less in the first three months of the year, according to the Spanberger campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Florida Rep. slams 'government-funded kidnapping program' after UF student is detained by ICE
item.description: A Florida Representative is calling out President Donald Trump and his administration for running a "government-funded kidnapping program" following ICE's recent detainment of a University of Florida student.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Democrats targeting 35 Republicans in 2026
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Latest: Trump expected to sign executive orders to boost coal
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to sign executive orders Tuesday aimed at boosting coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration plans to strip NYC of $188M for shelter program: Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The Trump administration is planning to strip New York City of $188 million, Mayor Eric Adams announced on Tuesday. The $188 million includes more than $80 million that the Federal Emergency Management Agency clawed back from the city in February.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Growing opposition from House conservatives threatens to derail Trump's agenda
item.description: Growing opposition among hard-line House conservatives to the Senate’s framework for advancing President Trump’s ambitious legislative agenda is threatening to make this week’s vote one of the heaviest lifts yet for Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.).
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says high tariffs may have prevented the Great Depression. History says different
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the early days of the Great Depression, Rep. Willis Hawley, a Republican from Oregon, and Utah Republican Sen. Reed Smoot thought they had landed on a way to protect American farmers and manufacturers from foreign competition: tariffs.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump tariffs about renegotiating trade deals, not jobs: Kasparian
item.description: Kasparian doesn't think Trump's tariffs are aimed at bringing factory jobs back despite what the president has said.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Will Trump tariffs bring jobs back to the US? Economists are skeptical
item.description: Economists on both sides of the aisle are skeptical that Trump's tariffs will lead to more factory jobs in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Fired Justice Department pardon attorney accuses the agency of 'ongoing corruption,' abuse of power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department's recently fired pardon attorney accused the leadership of the law enforcement agency of “ongoing corruption," testifying Monday at a congressional hearing meant to showcase concerns that the Trump administration is assaulting the rule of law, abusing its pow
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Congress expresses growing concern on Trump's tariffs
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties on Capitol Hill are voicing growing concern as President Donald Trump's tariffs wreak havoc on Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Roberts temporarily halts deadline to return mistakenly deported man
item.description: Chief Roberts agreed to hold the deadline until the high court can resolve the Trump administration’s emergency request to lift the judge’s order.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Proposed California bill seeks to extend last call until 4 a.m. in downtown areas
item.description: A new California bill would allow bars and restaurants in designated hospitality zones to serve alcohol until 4 a.m. on certain days, beginning Jan. 1, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Senator says he was joking about using violence against journalists
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., suggested that a lot "less false stories" would be written by journalists if they got into physical altercations with politicians.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit. But now they are faced with losing the biggest export market for many of their crops after China retaliated against President Donald Trump’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
